headers.comment: Default for every path (all SPA routes resolve to index.html): never cache the HTML entry point. A stale index.html references hashed chunk filenames that no longer exist after a deploy, which breaks lazy-loaded routes. The /assets/** rule below overrides this for fingerprinted files.

headers.value: default-src 'self'; base-uri 'self'; object-src 'none'; frame-ancestors 'none'; form-action 'self'; img-src 'self' data: https://images.unsplash.com https://*.googleusercontent.com; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; script-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com https://*.googleapis.com https://*.firebaseio.com https://*.firebaseapp.com

headers.comment: Listed LAST so it overrides the no-cache default: build assets are content-hashed, so a change yields a new filename and the old one can be cached indefinitely.
